clause_text: The Company shall deduct 12% of the Employee's basic wages (including dearness allowance and retaining allowance, if any) as the employee's contribution towards the Employees' Provident Fund and shall make a matching contribution of 12% as the employer's share, in accordance with Section 6 of the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952 and the Scheme framed thereunder. Both contributions shall be remitted to the Regional Provident Fund Commissioner by the 15th of the following month along with the prescribed ECR return. The Employee's Universal Account Number (UAN) shall be generated and intimated to the Employee within 30 days of joining.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: Since the Employee's gross monthly wages are ₹18,500 which is below the statutory wage ceiling of ₹21,000 prescribed under the Employees' State Insurance Act, 1948, the Company shall deduct 0.75% of wages as the employee's contribution and shall contribute 3.25% of wages as the employer's share towards ESIC in accordance with Section 39 of the said Act read with Regulation 31 of the ESI (General) Regulations, 1950. The contributions shall be deposited through the ESIC portal within 15 days of the close of every wage period. The Employee shall be entitled to all medical and cash benefits under the ESI Scheme through the dispensary allotted by the Regional Office.
indian_law: ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Employee agrees that the Cost-to-Company (CTC) of ₹6,00,000 per annum is all-inclusive and that both the employer's share and the employee's share of EPF contribution (aggregating to 24% of PF wages) shall be deducted from the said CTC. The Employee acknowledges that any statutory revision in contribution rates or wage ceilings shall be similarly absorbed within the stated CTC without any upward adjustment to gross salary. Monthly take-home pay shall accordingly stand reduced by such contributions.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The parties expressly agree and record that the Consultant is engaged on a principal-to-principal basis and is not an employee of the Company. Accordingly, no contributions towards Provident Fund under the EPF Act, 1952 or Employees' State Insurance under the ESI Act, 1948 shall be payable by the Company, and the Consultant hereby waives any right or claim to such statutory benefits. The Consultant shall bear sole responsibility for his/her own social security arrangements.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: Statutory deductions towards the Employees' Provident Fund (12% employee + 12% employer) and the Employees' State Insurance (0.75% employee + 3.25% employer, where applicable) shall be made from the Employee's monthly salary and remitted to the respective authorities in accordance with the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952 (Section 6) and the Employees' State Insurance Act, 1948 (Section 39). The Company shall issue Form 16 and the annual PF passbook entries through the EPFO Member e-Sewa portal. Coverage under ESIC shall cease once the Employee's wages cross the prescribed ceiling in accordance with the contribution period rules.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Company, being a principal employer within the meaning of Section 2(17) of the ESI Act, 1948, shall ensure that all contract labour deployed through the Contractor at its premises are covered under the ESI Scheme and the EPF Scheme. Should the Contractor fail to deposit PF/ESIC contributions in respect of such workmen, the Company reserves the right to recover the defaulted amount from pending bills and deposit the same directly with the authorities, without prejudice to its rights under Section 21 of the Contract Labour (Regulation and Abolition) Act, 1970.
indian_law: ESI Act 1948 Section 39; EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Employee's salary has been structured as a 'consolidated monthly compensation' of ₹45,000, and for purposes of PF contribution, the 'basic wages' component shall be deemed to be ₹15,000 only, being the statutory wage ceiling. Contributions under the EPF Act, 1952 shall accordingly be computed on ₹15,000 and no higher amount, irrespective of the actual wages drawn by the Employee. The Employee acknowledges and accepts this salary structuring.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: On completion of 5 (five) years of continuous service with the Company, the Employee shall be entitled to gratuity calculated at the rate of 15 days' wages (based on last drawn basic + DA) for every completed year of service, in accordance with Section 4 of the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972, subject to the statutory ceiling of ₹20,00,000. The Company has obtained a group gratuity policy with LIC to discharge this obligation. Nomination under Form F shall be obtained from the Employee at the time of joining.
indian_law: Gratuity Act 1972 Section 4

clause_text: Notwithstanding that the Employee draws a gross monthly salary of ₹19,800 and is otherwise a 'covered employee' within the meaning of Section 2(9) of the ESI Act, 1948, the parties agree that ESIC shall not be applicable to this engagement and the Employee shall make his own arrangements for medical insurance. The Employee confirms that he has no objection to such arrangement and shall not raise any claim against the Company in this regard.
indian_law: ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: Contributions towards the Employees' Provident Fund shall be made by the Company and the Employee at the rate of 12% each of the Employee's basic salary plus dearness allowance, as mandated by Section 6 of the EPF & MP Act, 1952. The Company's contribution shall be apportioned between the EPF (3.67%) and the Employees' Pension Scheme, 1995 (8.33%, capped at ₹1,250 per month on a pensionable salary of ₹15,000). All contributions shall be remitted via the Electronic Challan-cum-Return (ECR) by the 15th of the succeeding month.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Employee hereby acknowledges that the Company's contribution towards the Employees' Provident Fund is a component of the stated CTC of ₹12,00,000 per annum and does not represent an additional benefit over and above the gross salary. In the event the Employee opts out of PF coverage (for those with starting basic wages exceeding ₹15,000 per month who have never been an EPF member), the equivalent sum shall remain with the Company and shall not be paid to the Employee in any form. This structuring is at the sole discretion of the Management.

The Employee hereby acknowledges that the Company's contribution towards the Employees' Provident Fund forms part of the CTC of ₹12,00,000 per annum. Should the Employee exercise the option to not enrol in EPF (being an 'excluded employee' under Para 2(f) of the EPF Scheme, 1952 with starting basic wages exceeding ₹15,000), the corresponding notional sum shall stand forfeited and shall not be paid out in any alternate form. Such structuring is at the sole discretion of the Management.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: M/s Lotus Software Services Pvt. Ltd. being an establishment employing more than 20 persons is a 'covered establishment' under Section 1(3) of the EPF & MP Act, 1952 bearing EPFO Code No. DLCPM1234567000. The Employee shall be enrolled as a member of the Fund from the date of joining and contributions shall be remitted as per Section 6 of the said Act. The Employee is advised to update his/her KYC details (Aadhaar, PAN, bank account) on the Unified Member Portal to ensure seamless transfer and withdrawal facilities.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Consultant is engaged as an independent professional on a retainership basis for a monthly fee of ₹55,000 and shall raise GST-compliant invoices. The parties confirm that no employer-employee relationship subsists between them, and accordingly, the provisions of the EPF Act, 1952, the ESI Act, 1948 and the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972 shall not apply to this engagement. The Consultant shall be solely responsible for his income-tax, professional tax and any social security contributions he may choose to make in his individual capacity.

The Consultant is engaged as an independent professional on retainership for a monthly fee of ₹55,000 and shall raise GST-compliant invoices. No employer-employee relationship subsists between the parties, and accordingly the EPF Act 1952, ESI Act 1948 and Payment of Gratuity Act 1972 shall not apply. The Consultant shall be solely responsible for her income-tax, professional tax and any social security contributions in her individual capacity.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: Aarogya Biotech LLP, being a 'covered establishment' under the EPF & MP Act, 1952, shall enrol the Employee as a member of the Employees' Provident Fund with effect from the date of joining. Contributions of 12% each on PF wages (basic + DA + retaining allowance) shall be deducted and matched by the LLP pursuant to Section 6 of the Act. Administrative charges of 0.5% and EDLI contribution of 0.5% (capped as per Rule) shall be borne wholly by the employer. The Employee's UAN shall be linked to Aadhaar within 30 days.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: For the period during which the Employee's gross wages remain at or below ₹21,000 per month, the Company shall contribute 3.25% and deduct 0.75% of such wages towards the Employees' State Insurance Scheme under Section 39 of the ESI Act, 1948. Upon the Employee's wages exceeding the wage ceiling during the course of a contribution period, contributions shall continue until the end of the contribution period in which such crossing occurs, in accordance with Regulation 40 of the ESI (General) Regulations, 1950.
indian_law: ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: TCS Retail Solutions Pvt. Ltd. shall comply in full with the provisions of the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952 and the Employees' State Insurance Act, 1948. The Employee authorises the Company to deduct the statutory employee share of 12% towards EPF and 0.75% towards ESIC (subject to wage ceiling) from his monthly wages. Employer shares at the prescribed rates shall be borne by the Company over and above the stated CTC. All filings shall be made on the Shram Suvidha unified portal.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Principal Employer shall verify each month, before releasing payments to the Contractor, that the Contractor has (i) deposited PF contributions in respect of every deployed workman under its own EPFO code, and (ii) remitted ESIC contributions under Section 39 of the ESI Act, 1948. Proof of such remittance shall be submitted along with the monthly RA bill. Section 21(4) of the Contract Labour (Regulation and Abolition) Act, 1970 shall apply in case of any default by the Contractor.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Employee is hereby informed that Nexonova India Pvt. Ltd. (an MNC subsidiary) has enrolled the establishment with EPFO vide Code MHBAN0456789000 and with ESIC vide Code 31000456780000999. Statutory contributions shall be deducted and deposited in accordance with Section 6 of the EPF Act, 1952 and Section 39 of the ESI Act, 1948. The Employee shall be eligible for Employees' Deposit-Linked Insurance (EDLI) benefits up to ₹7,00,000 in the unfortunate event of death during service.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: Upon superannuation, resignation or retirement after completing not less than 5 years of continuous service, the Employee shall be entitled to payment of gratuity under Section 4 of the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972 calculated at 15 days' wages for each completed year of service or part thereof in excess of 6 months. Such gratuity shall be paid within 30 days of it becoming payable, failing which simple interest at the rate notified by the Central Government shall accrue.
indian_law: Gratuity Act 1972 Section 4

clause_text: As the Company is a 'factory' within the meaning of Section 2(12) of the ESI Act, 1948 employing 23 workers, ESIC registration is mandatory. Every employee drawing wages of ₹21,000 per month or less shall be covered, and contributions at 0.75% (employee) and 3.25% (employer) shall be remitted by the 15th of the following month. Medical benefit to the insured Employee and his dependents shall be provided from the date of entering insurable employment, in accordance with the ESI (General) Regulations, 1950.
indian_law: ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Company shall contribute 8.33% of the Employee's PF wages (subject to the pensionable salary ceiling of ₹15,000 per month) towards the Employees' Pension Scheme, 1995 out of its 12% employer share, with the balance 3.67% credited to the EPF account. This apportionment is as mandated under paragraph 3(2) of the EPS, 1995 read with Section 6A of the EPF & MP Act, 1952. The Employee shall be eligible for monthly member pension on attaining 58 years of age subject to minimum 10 years of eligible service.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: Infra Power Construction LLP, being the principal employer on the site at Dahej SEZ, Gujarat, undertakes to obtain a separate sub-code with EPFO for contract labour engaged through Skilled Hands Manpower Pvt. Ltd. Contributions shall be computed on the actual wages paid to each workman in accordance with Section 6 of the EPF Act, 1952 and the Basic Wages definition as clarified by the Hon'ble Supreme Court in Vivekananda Vidyamandir (2019). No component of wages shall be artificially split to reduce the PF base.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The monthly pay slip issued to the Employee shall separately disclose: (a) employee's EPF contribution at 12%; (b) employer's EPF contribution at 12% (of which 8.33% is towards EPS); (c) employee's ESIC contribution at 0.75% (if applicable); and (d) employer's ESIC contribution at 3.25% (if applicable). This disclosure is in compliance with Rule 10 of the Wages Rules under the Code on Wages, 2019 and Section 6 of the EPF Act, 1952.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: International transfer of PF accumulations: In case the Employee is an International Worker within the meaning of Para 83 of the EPF Scheme, 1952, contributions shall be made on full monthly pay without application of the ₹15,000 wage ceiling, in accordance with the amendment dated 01.10.2008. The Company shall also facilitate social security totalisation benefits under bilateral Social Security Agreements (SSA) entered into by the Government of India with the Employee's home country, where applicable.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: As a gig-economy platform, Swiftly Tech Pvt. Ltd. acknowledges the obligations under Section 114 of the Code on Social Security, 2020 (when notified) and has voluntarily registered its platform workers on the e-Shram portal. Until the Code is fully operationalised, the Company shall continue to extend EPF coverage under Section 6 of the EPF Act, 1952 to those on-roll employees who are not gig workers, and contribute to the Social Security Fund for gig workers at the rate notified by the Central Government.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Employee's contribution of 12% shall be deducted from the wages payable for each wage period and, together with the Company's matching contribution of 12%, shall be deposited into the Employee's individual PF account maintained with EPFO. Any delay in deposit shall attract interest at 12% per annum under Section 7Q and damages up to 25% per annum under Section 14B of the EPF & MP Act, 1952, which shall be borne exclusively by the Company.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: During maternity leave availed by the Employee under the Maternity Benefit Act, 1961, the Company shall continue to remit the full employee and employer contributions towards EPF (24% in aggregate) on the basis of the notional wages that would have been paid but for such leave. ESIC contributions shall also continue as per Section 39 of the ESI Act, 1948, and maternity benefit under the ESI Scheme shall be available to the insured Employee in lieu of the Maternity Benefit Act where so elected.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: On cessation of employment, the Employee may either (i) withdraw the accumulated PF balance by filing Form 19 and Form 10C with the Regional PF Commissioner after a waiting period of 2 months (where unemployed), or (ii) transfer the accumulations to the new employer's PF account via Form 13 / Online Transfer Claim Portal (OTCP). The Company shall issue the full and final settlement and Form 16 within 45 days of the last working day.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: Voluntary Provident Fund (VPF): The Employee may, by written election submitted to HR, contribute an additional amount over and above the statutory 12% towards his PF account, subject to a maximum of 100% of basic + DA. Such VPF contribution shall not impose any matching obligation on the Company and shall be governed by Para 26(6) of the EPF Scheme, 1952. The Employee acknowledges that interest accretion on aggregate contributions above ₹2.5 lakh per year will be taxable under Section 10(11)/(12) of the Income-tax Act, 1961.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Contractor shall submit, on or before the 20th of every month, certified copies of the ECR challan (EPF) and the ESIC Challan along with the list of workmen deployed at the Principal Employer's premises, together with wage registers in Form XVIII under Rule 78 of the CLRA Central Rules, 1971. Non-submission shall entitle the Principal Employer to withhold 10% of the contract value and deposit equivalent contributions directly with EPFO/ESIC under Section 8A of the EPF Act, 1952.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: Where the Employee's wages are revised during a contribution period such that they cross the ESIC ceiling of ₹21,000 per month, ESIC contributions shall nonetheless continue until 31st March / 30th September, as the case may be (being the end of the ongoing contribution period), in terms of Regulation 40 and 4 of the ESI (General) Regulations, 1950. Thereafter, the Employee shall cease to be covered under the Scheme and no contribution shall be deducted.
indian_law: ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: Sri Ganesh Textile Mills Pvt. Ltd., Tirupur shall cover all workmen including apprentices (other than apprentices under the Apprentices Act, 1961) under the EPF and ESI Schemes. Contributions shall be made as per Section 6 of the EPF Act, 1952 and Section 39 of the ESI Act, 1948 and shall be remitted through the unified Shram Suvidha portal. The Company shall display the abstract of both Acts at a conspicuous place in the factory as required by law.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Employee shall, within 15 days of joining, complete Form 11 (Declaration by a Person Taking Up Employment), Form 2 (Nomination and Declaration) and Form 1 / 1A under the ESIC (Family declaration). The Company shall register and issue the UAN activation intimation and the ESIC e-pehchan card within 21 days. All statutory contributions shall be remitted under Section 6 of the EPF Act, 1952 and Section 39 of the ESI Act, 1948.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Employee acknowledges that the employer's contribution to EPF, EPS and EDLI, administrative charges, gratuity provision and ESIC employer share are components forming part of the stated CTC purely for disclosure purposes, but such employer obligations shall in no event be deducted from the Employee's gross salary. All statutory contributions shall be made strictly as per Section 6 of the EPF Act, 1952 and Section 39 of the ESI Act, 1948 and shall be borne by the Company.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: Consultants engaged by Vedanta Analytics Pvt. Ltd. who satisfy the tests laid down by the Hon'ble Supreme Court in Silver Jubilee Tailoring House and subsequent rulings (integration test, control test, economic reality test) and are found to be 'employees' in substance shall be enrolled under the EPF and ESI Schemes notwithstanding the nomenclature of the agreement. Such coverage shall be with retrospective effect from the date of commencement of the engagement, with arrears deposited along with interest under Section 7Q of the EPF Act, 1952.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: In the event the establishment ceases to employ 20 or more persons, the Company shall continue to remain covered under Section 1(5) of the EPF & MP Act, 1952 (infection clause) and contributions shall continue to be remitted for all existing members. No member already enrolled shall be denied the benefit of continued membership by reason of subsequent reduction in employment strength.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: Contributions under the EPF Act, 1952 shall be calculated on the 'basic wages' as defined in Section 2(b) of the Act, read with the law laid down in Regional Provident Fund Commissioner (II), West Bengal v. Vivekananda Vidyamandir (2019) 2 SCC 630, whereby all allowances which are universally, necessarily and ordinarily paid to all employees are includible in 'basic wages' for PF. The Company undertakes to structure wages in conformity with this ruling.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: For apprentices engaged under the Apprentices Act, 1961, no contribution towards EPF or ESIC shall be payable in accordance with Section 2(f)(ii) of the EPF Act, 1952 and Section 2(9)(b) of the ESI Act, 1948. However, upon absorption on the regular roll of the Company, such apprentices shall be enrolled as members of both Schemes with effect from the date of absorption.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Employee is placed on deputation from the parent Company (M/s Global Services GmbH, Germany) to its Indian subsidiary. As an International Worker within the meaning of Para 2(ff) of the EPF Scheme, 1952, contributions shall be made on the full salary without application of the ₹15,000 wage ceiling, in accordance with Section 6 of the EPF Act, 1952. Withdrawal shall be governed by Para 69(6) of the Scheme and by the India-Germany Social Security Agreement.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Gig Worker is engaged by Zypp Delivery Pvt. Ltd. on the platform. Pursuant to Section 114 of the Code on Social Security, 2020, upon notification, the Company shall contribute between 1% and 2% of its annual turnover (capped at 5% of payments to gig workers) to the Social Security Fund for such workers. Until notification, the Company shall register the Gig Worker on e-Shram and facilitate access to PM-Shram Yogi Maandhan Yojana.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Company, being registered under Section 2A of the EPF & MP Act, 1952 as a single establishment covering its head office at Bengaluru and branch offices at Hyderabad and Pune, shall deposit consolidated PF contributions under one EPFO Code. The Employee, irrespective of posting location, shall remain a member of the same PF account, and transfer claims shall not be required on inter-branch transfer.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Employee's monthly compensation is structured as ₹15,000 basic + ₹40,000 special allowance + ₹10,000 house rent allowance + ₹8,000 conveyance. PF contributions under the EPF Act, 1952 shall be computed on basic wages of ₹15,000 only. The Employee accepts that the special allowance has been designed by the Company as a non-PF component and waives any objection in relation to computation of PF wages.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The employer's PF contribution, EDLI and administrative charges are included in and form part of the stated CTC of ₹8,50,000 per annum. Any future increase in the statutory contribution rates, wage ceiling or inclusion of additional allowances in PF wages (including pursuant to judicial pronouncements) shall be absorbed within the existing CTC without any corresponding increase in gross or take-home salary.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: Coverage under the Employees' State Insurance Scheme shall be subject to the Company's administrative convenience and the availability of ESIC dispensaries in the Employee's work location. Where the Management is of the view that such coverage is impractical or the Employee is deputed to a non-implemented area, ESIC contributions shall be suspended and the Employee shall make his own arrangements.
indian_law: ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Employee shall bear and pay both the employee's and the employer's share of EPF contribution (aggregating to 24% of PF wages) by way of deduction from monthly gross salary. The Company, being desirous of keeping its own wage cost competitive, has passed on its matching liability to the Employee through this arrangement, to which the Employee consents.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: Gratuity, PF and ESIC contributions shall be payable only at the sole discretion of the Management and subject to the financial performance of the Company in the relevant financial year. The Management reserves the right to suspend or reduce such contributions where business conditions warrant, without giving rise to any claim or demand by the Employee.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Employee is engaged on a 'consolidated wage' of ₹28,000 per month, which shall constitute the sole and entire remuneration payable. PF contributions, if any, shall be computed by treating 50% of such consolidated wage (i.e. ₹14,000) as 'basic wages' for purposes of Section 6 of the EPF Act, 1952. The balance 50% shall be treated as allowances not attracting PF.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Employee agrees that upon separation, the Company shall recover from the final settlement amount any employer's share of PF deposited during the Employee's tenure, in case the Employee has served for less than 24 months. The Employee irrevocably authorises such recovery and further agrees that the Company's contribution to EPS shall stand forfeited in such case.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Company shall enrol the Employee under EPF only after successful completion of probation (12 months). During the probation period, no contributions towards EPF or ESIC shall be made, and the Employee shall have no claim in respect of such period, including for pension, EDLI or medical benefit.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The stated CTC includes a notional 'PF provision' of ₹21,600 per annum. In the event the Employee is an 'excluded employee' under Para 2(f) of the EPF Scheme or otherwise opts out of PF, this notional amount shall stand forfeited in favour of the Company and shall not be paid out in cash or as any other benefit.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: Compensation is structured with basic wages of ₹14,500 per month (below the PF wage ceiling of ₹15,000) specifically to minimise the Company's statutory contribution liability. The balance components (special allowance, performance pay, site allowance, attendance bonus) are expressly designed as non-PF components and the Employee acknowledges and accepts such structuring.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: For Employees drawing gross wages up to ₹21,000 per month, the Company shall, at its discretion, either enrol them under ESIC or provide a private group mediclaim policy of an equivalent sum insured in lieu thereof. Where the latter option is exercised, no contribution shall be made to ESIC notwithstanding Section 39 of the ESI Act, 1948.
indian_law: ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: In the event of any retrospective demand by EPFO or ESIC on account of inclusion of additional allowances in PF/ESI wages, short-payment of contributions, or misclassification of employees, the entire amount of such demand (including employer's share, damages under Section 14B and interest under Section 7Q) shall be recoverable from the Employee by way of deduction from salary or final settlement.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Employee is engaged as a 'trainee' on a monthly stipend of ₹24,000 for a period of 24 months, during which no PF, ESIC or gratuity benefits shall accrue. The engagement shall be governed by the company's internal 'Trainee Policy' and not by the EPF Act, 1952 or the ESI Act, 1948, notwithstanding that the training is not under the Apprentices Act, 1961.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Employee is hired on a 'fixed-term' contract of 11 months. Given the short duration, the Company shall treat the Employee as a 'casual worker' and shall not enrol him under EPF or ESIC. On completion/renewal, a fresh 11-month contract shall be executed so as to preserve this arrangement.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Employee acknowledges that while statutory PF contributions shall be deposited, the 'pensionable salary' for EPS purposes shall be restricted to ₹15,000 per month regardless of higher actual wages, and the Employee hereby waives any right to claim pension on higher wages pursuant to the EPFO v. Sunil Kumar (2022) judgment or otherwise. No joint option under Para 11(3) of EPS, 1995 shall be entertained.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: The Consultant / Independent Contractor engagement fee of ₹60,000 per month is inclusive of all taxes, statutory levies and social security costs. Should any authority reclassify this engagement as 'employment' and raise demands under the EPF Act, 1952 or ESI Act, 1948, the Consultant shall fully indemnify the Company against such demands including interest and damages, and the Company may set off such amounts against fees payable.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: All statutory employer contributions (PF 12%, ESIC 3.25%, gratuity provision, bonus, LWF) have been 'grossed up' into the Employee's CTC of ₹4,20,000. The Employee's 'take-home' salary of ₹28,500 per month has been arrived at after deducting both employee and employer contributions from the monthly CTC of ₹35,000, and the Employee consents to this computation.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: During lay-off, strike, lockout or other involuntary absence, no PF or ESIC contribution shall be deposited by the Company in respect of the affected Employee, and the Employee shall not be entitled to any back-contribution upon resumption of work. This shall apply notwithstanding any continuing service relationship under industrial legislation.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: In the event the Employee is found to have simultaneous employment or dual UAN, the Company reserves the right to stop PF contributions forthwith and to recover all contributions previously deposited on behalf of the Employee, without requiring regularization by EPFO. The Employee hereby indemnifies the Company against any consequence thereof.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: For the purpose of computing gratuity under the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972, the Employee's 'wages' shall be deemed to mean only the basic salary of ₹15,000 per month, excluding dearness allowance or any other allowance, notwithstanding anything to the contrary in Section 2(s) of the said Act. The Employee expressly consents to this computation basis.
indian_law: Gratuity Act 1972 Section 4

clause_text: The Company shall, at its sole discretion, review the Employee's continuance in the PF scheme annually. Where the Employee's PF wages exceed ₹15,000 per month after annual increment, the Employer may cap its matching contribution at ₹1,800 per month (i.e. 12% of ₹15,000) and shall not be obliged to contribute on actual higher wages.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6

clause_text: Contract labour deployed under this Agreement shall be covered under EPF/ESIC solely by the Contractor under its own code. The Principal Employer shall have no direct or vicarious liability under Section 8A of the EPF Act, 1952 or Section 40 of the ESI Act, 1948 in the event of default by the Contractor, and the Contractor shall indemnify the Principal Employer against any such claim.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: The Platform Partner engaged by Gigwork Mobility Pvt. Ltd. expressly agrees that no statutory contributions (PF, ESIC, gratuity, bonus) shall be payable by the Company in any form, notwithstanding use of the Company's branded uniform, app-based allocation of tasks, deactivation rights or KYC onboarding. The Platform Partner shall bear the entire cost of his/her own social security.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

The Company shall provide the Employee with a monthly salary slip showing the break-up of basic wages, dearness allowance, house rent allowance and other components, along with the statutory deductions towards EPF, ESIC and professional tax.  All contributions referred to herein shall be in addition to, and not in substitution of, the gross salary payable to the Employee as mentioned in the Appointment Letter.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: It is mutually agreed between Infotrend Technologies Pvt. Ltd., engaged in the business of healthcare with operations at Bhubaneswar, and Sanjay Vaidya that The Company shall deduct twelve per cent (12%) of the Employee's basic wages (inclusive of dearness allowance and retaining allowance, if any) as the Employee's share of the contribution to the Employees' Provident Fund and shall remit the same, along with a matching employer contribution of twelve per cent (12%), to the Regional Provident Fund Commissioner within fifteen (15) days of the close of every wage period.  The Employer shall ensure that the Employee's Aadhaar and PAN are seeded with the UAN, and shall provide assistance in KYC updation and nomination under the Employees' Provident Fund Scheme, 1952.  Any statutory revision in the rates of contribution, wage ceilings, or eligibility thresholds shall apply automatically from the effective date of the notification, and the parties' obligations shall be adjusted accordingly.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: Pursuant to the engagement of Sanjay Joshi by Shree Foods Pvt. Ltd. at its media and entertainment operations at Coimbatore, the Cost-to-Company (CTC) of Rs. 19,00,000/- per annum per annum is all-inclusive and the Employee agrees that both the employer's share and the Employee's share of contributions to the Employees' Provident Fund (aggregating to 24% of PF wages) shall be deducted from the said CTC.  Bonus under the Payment of Bonus Act, 1965 shall be payable only where computation of allocable surplus so warrants, and the Employer shall be entitled to set off any ex-gratia or performance pay already paid against the statutory bonus liability.  The Employee agrees that the Employer's periodic revision of the break-up of gross salary, to align with changes in judicial interpretation of 'basic wages', shall not be treated as a reduction of salary or adverse variation of service conditions.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: It is hereby recorded that with reference to this appointment letter executed between Infotrend Engineering Pvt. Ltd. at Lucknow and Sonal Banerjee, engaged in the capacity of a professional in the renewable energy sector, all allowances payable to the Employee, including house rent allowance, conveyance allowance, special allowance and performance pay, shall be structured to fall outside the definition of 'basic wages' under the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952, so as to limit the Employer's contribution obligation.  The Employer shall not be liable to pay any interest, damages or penalty under Section 7Q or 14B of the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952 arising from late remittance, and such liability shall be borne by the Employee to the extent attributable to any delay.  The Employee agrees that the Employer's periodic revision of the break-up of gross salary, to align with changes in judicial interpretation of 'basic wages', shall not be treated as a reduction of salary or adverse variation of service conditions.

clause_text: The Employer may, at its discretion, reclassify the Employee's engagement as that of a 'retainer' or 'consultant' to avoid the liability to contribute to the Employees' Provident Fund, and the Employee consents to such reclassification on prior notice.  The Employee's gratuity contribution shall be limited to the amount payable under the statutory formula, and no amount beyond the statutory ceiling shall be payable on cessation of employment, notwithstanding the Employee's total tenure of service.  Any challenge raised by the Employee to the statutory classification of allowances, or the treatment of CTC as inclusive of statutory contributions, shall be treated as a material breach of this contract labour agreement.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: Employee stock option plans, retention bonuses and variable pay shall be structured as separate allowances outside the definition of 'wages' under the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952, and no contribution shall be made in respect of such amounts.  The Employer may, on its own election, opt out of the ESIC scheme in respect of the Employee on the ground that the Employee is covered by an alternative group medical insurance policy, notwithstanding the mandatory nature of the ESIC Act, 1948.  The Employee agrees that the Employer's periodic revision of the break-up of gross salary, to align with changes in judicial interpretation of 'basic wages', shall not be treated as a reduction of salary or adverse variation of service conditions.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: All allowances payable to the Employee, including house rent allowance, conveyance allowance, special allowance and performance pay, shall be structured to fall outside the definition of 'basic wages' under the Employees' Provident Funds and Miscellaneous Provisions Act, 1952, so as to limit the Employer's contribution obligation.  The Employee's gratuity contribution shall be limited to the amount payable under the statutory formula, and no amount beyond the statutory ceiling shall be payable on cessation of employment, notwithstanding the Employee's total tenure of service.  The Employer shall have the right to recover from the Employee, through deduction from salary, any sum paid by the Employer on account of retrospective statutory demand attributable to the Employee's engagement.
indian_law: EPF Act 1952 Section 6; ESI Act 1948 Section 39

clause_text: With effect from the date of execution of this employment contract at Kochi between Aarogya Insurance Brokers Pvt. Bonus under the Payment of Bonus Act, 1965 shall be payable only where computation of allocable surplus so warrants, and the Employer shall be entitled to set off any ex-gratia or performance pay already paid against the statutory bonus liability.  The Employer shall have the right to recover from the Employee, through deduction from salary, any sum paid by the Employer on account of retrospective statutory demand attributable to the Employee's engagement.
